summary: The suite completely omits unit tests for all four core modules (version.ts, commits.ts, changelog.ts, and the file-reading helpers in main.ts). Every business-logic requirement — parseVersion with 'v' prefix, bumpVersion boundary conditions, parseCommit edge cases, determineBumpType priority ordering, generateChangelog formatting, package.json as version source, the 'no bump needed' path, and all error-handling branches — is exercised only indirectly through three act integration runs or not at all. The workflow structure tests are solid and the act integration tests correctly assert exact version strings and bump-type labels for all three happy-path scenarios, which is good. However the lack of any unit-level tests means a bug introduced in parseCommit or determineBumpType could easily survive these tests if the integration scaffold happens to mask it. Minor design issues: cleanup (rmSync) occurs inside the test body so a mid-test failure leaks temp directories, and the Bun.spawnSync timeout value is commented as nanoseconds but the API expects milliseconds (300_000_000_000 ms ≈ 9.5 years). The describe/beforeAll structure and helper abstractions (setupTestRepo, runAct, appendResult) are well-organised.
